ksa_text: Conduct fundamental rights impact assessments for AI systems, including rights identification, disproportionate impact analysis, and integration of assessment findings into deployment decisions.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Included: task maps directly to AI Ethics Assessor scope — RSK-specific signals detected ("impact assess", "fundamental rights.{0,10} ?:assess|impact", "fundamental.{0,10}rights?"). STRM composite is zero (0.0000 — note: STRM frameworks underweight pure risk vocabulary); note that framework scores routinely underweight risk management and governance vocabulary, so text-based signal carries primary weight here. Seniority is appropriate: the KSA describes practice-level execution, not enterprise strategy.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External check: no matches in RSK job postings or cert curricula (CRISC, FRM, NIST AI RMF, CISM). Would a working AI Ethics Assessor self-report this as required?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Challenge answered: the competency is implicit in AI Ethics Assessor work (domain affinity 0.90, 3 RSK signal(s)). Framework vocabulary matching systematically underrepresents risk management and governance KSAs.
